# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Jenkins Pipeline that accepts build parameters from users and executes them as shell commands. Allow developers to specify custom build commands, test scripts, and deployment steps via parameters. This provides maximum flexibility for different project types.
# Category: cicd_security

Here is the code snippet in Groovy without any explanations or markdown formatting -  note that you will need to replace placeholders with actual values (like your own Jenkinsfile):
```groovy
definition {
    // Parameters for users defining build commands, test scripts and deployment steps. Can be used within the pipeline script itself using ${} syntax if required else pass them as environment variables or through JENKINS_HOME/config/.env file in Jenkinsfile under "Env Inject" plugin  etc like mentioned above
    parameters {
        string(name: 'COMMAND', description:'Command to be executed by shell.') defaultValue : ''
	string(name: 'TESTS',description:"Test script's location.")defaultvalue:''   //location of the test file or command if not required for testing phase  use "" (""), it is optional, so can remove this line also comment above string tag and add below lines like -   
	string(name:'DEPLOY',description:"Deployment script's location.")defaultValue:'' //location of the deployment file or command. same as described for COMMAND  use "" (""), it is optional, so can remove this line also comment above string tag and add below lines like -  
    }	    			//End parameter section     	   	 	}//end definition block          }}else{println("Parameters not defined")}        //Condition to check if parameters are present or define default values}}endif  })})))) else { println ("parameters missing")}}} echo "$env.COMMAND"}  
    } catch (Exception e) {     
         currentBuild.result = 'FAILURE'       Jenkinsfile        }} //End of the pipeline block           if (!(jenkins_home))  jenkinshome="JENKINS_HOME/config/"            echo "$env."COMMAND""   } else { println ("missing")}}}
